CD4⁺ Perturb-seq · Melanoma ICB · Interactive lookup

Gene explorer — the CD4⁺ ICB-resistance state

Type a gene symbol to see whether it moves up or down in the non-responder resistance state, and whether it belongs to a regulatory pathway — as a regulator, as a downstream target, or both.

🔍
Try:
Start typing above, or pick an example gene.

How to read the result

Resistance signature. Per-gene differential expression, non-responder vs responder (DESeq2, melanoma tumor CD4). Up in non-responder = part of the resistance state; up in responder = part of the response state. Significance is padj < 0.05; 433 of 24,416 genes clear it in this cohort.

Regulator role. If the gene was knocked down in the Perturb-seq atlas, its model coefficient says which state its knockdown reproduces: positive → resistance-reproducing (knockdown pushes cells toward non-responder), negative → response-reproducing. Pathway chips flag membership in the three curated regulatory pathways: SAGA/chromatin DUB, interferon, UFMylation.

Downstream target. Whether the gene is a significant target (10% FDR) of the SAGA or interferon pathway, how many pathway members control it, and the knockdown direction: KD raises vs KD lowers the gene.

Data: melanoma tumor CD4 R-vs-NR signature (Sade-Feldman GSE120575) + genome-scale CD4 Perturb-seq atlas. 24,667 genes indexed · 2,552 profiled regulators · 203 pathway targets.
Reproducible code & data · Claude Science
